value.user_value: A0 history QA Gate: proves the backfilled 2016+ A0 history is not just URLs but real, readable, versioned, and point-in-time-resolvable content -- readable attachments (real binary files verified by magic bytes + sha256), correct revision chains (idempotent re-observation makes no phantom version; a genuine body edit / status flip 现行有效->已废止 / attachment change each make exactly one new version), correct as-of resolution (a query never resolves to a future version), and 0 unexplained P0 coverage gaps.
value.why_it_matters: This is the quality gate that lets later stages (as-of history query base T056, province/city expansion T049-T051) trust the A0 corpus. Without it, 'we have the history' could silently mean 'we have URLs that 404'.

cost.note: unknown != 0; the QA gate ran deterministically over the corpus, and the attachment readback ran from the DEV environment (not the worker) -> 0 cloud cost, DIR-007 unaffected.
cost.human_maintenance: dev-env live attachment readback + deterministic gate authoring

deployment: NOT_DEPLOYED (QA gate; production worker/cron/data untouched, live build unchanged b189d3cc0703 == T040)
